arguments.name: build-catalog-app
arguments.prompt:
You are working in /tmp/sv-c55d6afcbffc45cd8ba20aa1c9c4d6ac/UniversityCourseCatalog, a .NET 8 Blazor Web App (server interactivity). Build a university course catalog application.

The project already exists with the standard template files. You need to:

1. Create a `Models` folder with the following model classes in `Models/CatalogModels.cs`:
   - `Department` (Id, Name, Code, Description)
   - `Course` (Id, DepartmentId, Title, Code, Description, Credits, Schedule, InstructorName, InstructorBio, IsFeatured)
   - `EnrollmentRequest` (Name, StudentId, CourseId) — used for the form

2. Create a `Services` folder with `Services/CatalogService.cs`:
   - A `CatalogService` class with seed data (at least 4 departments: Computer Science, Mathematics, Physics, English; at least 3 courses per department with realistic data)
   - Methods: GetDepartments(), GetDepartment(int id), GetCourses(), GetCoursesByDepartment(int deptId), GetCourse(int id), GetFeaturedCourses(), SearchCourses(string query), SubmitEnrollmentRequest(EnrollmentRequest request)
   - Register as a singleton in Program.cs

3. Update `Program.cs` to register the CatalogService as a singleton.

4. Replace `Components/Pages/Home.razor` with a homepage showing:
   - A hero section with university name and tagline
   - Featured courses section (cards with title, department, credits, link to detail)
   - Department links section

5. Create `Components/Pages/Courses.razor` (@page "/courses"):
   - Searchable, filterable course list
   - Text search input and department filter dropdown
   - Display courses as cards/list items with title, code, credits, department
   - Link each course to its detail page
   - Use interactive server rendering for the search/filter

6. Create `Components/Pages/CourseDetail.razor` (@page "/courses/{Id:int}"):
   - Show full course info: title, code, department, description, schedule, credits
   - Instructor section with name and bio
   - Link back to course list

7. Create `Components/Pages/Departments.razor` (@page "/departments"):
   - List all departments with description
   - Link to filtered course list for each department

8. Create `Components/Pages/Enroll.razor` (@page "/enroll"):
   - Standard HTML form with: Name (text), Student ID (text), Course selection (dropdown of all courses)
   - Form posts and shows a confirmation message on success
   - Use EditForm with standard validation (Required attributes)
   - Use interactive server rendering for the form

9. Update `Components/Layout/NavMenu.razor` to have navigation links: Home, Courses, Departments, Enroll

10. Update `Components/Layout/MainLayout.razor` if needed for a clean layout with header/footer.

11. Add some basic CSS to `wwwroot/app.css` (or the existing CSS file) for cards, hero section, and general styling that looks like a university website.

Delete the Counter.razor and Weather.razor pages as they are not needed.

Make sure all pages use `@using UniversityCourseCatalog.Models` and `@inject CatalogService` where needed. Add the using to `_Imports.razor`.

Use `@rendermode InteractiveServer` only on pages that need interactivity (Courses search/filter, Enroll form). Other pages can use static SSR.

The app targets .NET 9 (check the csproj for the actual TFM and use that).

The Blazor Web App is ready. Here's what was built:

**Structure:**
- `Models/CatalogModels.cs` — Department, Course, EnrollmentRequest
- `Services/CatalogService.cs` — Singleton with 4 departments, 13 courses, search/filter/enroll methods
- **Pages:** Home (`/`), Courses (`/courses`), CourseDetail (`/courses/{id}`), Departments (`/departments`), Enroll (`/enroll`)

**Key decisions:**
- Interactive Server rendering only on pages needing it (Courses filter, Enroll form)
- Static SSR for Home, Departments, CourseDetail (fast on intranet)
- University-themed CSS with hero section, cards, and form styling
- No authentication as requested

Run with `dotnet run` from the `UniversityCourseCatalog` directory.
